ocr.text: The following table presents the results of an ordinary least squares regression analysis examining the relationship between
cathepsin B levels and various independent variables, measured in mg/mg protein. The analysis incorporates data from two
distinct assessment methods: AT-AirView and King Vision, each with a sample size of 90 observations. Notably, the coefficients
and associated confidence intervals for each method are provided, highlighting the variation in cathepsin B levels across
different conditions. For instance, the coefficient for the AT-AirView method indicates a significant negative association with a
value of −0.0348 (95% CI: −0.0561, −0.0135), with a corresponding P value of 0.0014, suggesting that as the independent
variable increases, cathepsin B levels decrease.
Half-lives (h)  Content in lysosomes
(mg/mg protein)
Cathepsin B  14  50  330  920
Cathepsin H  14  30  280  600
Cathepsin L  24  100  105  510
(Control)  (E-64)  (Control)  (E-64)
90-day readmission  Barthel index at discharge  Log-transformed length of stay
Coefficient (95% CI)  P value  Coefficient (95% CI)  P value  Coefficient (95% CI)  P value
Hausman specification test  0.956  0.339  < 0.0001
Ease of device insertion  Ease of epiglottis visualisation  Ease of intubation
AT-AirViewn = 90  King Visionn = 90  AT-AirViewn = 90  King Visionn = 90  AT-AirViewn = 90  King Visionn = 90
In contrast, the King Vision method presents a different coefficient, emphasizing the importance of assessing the method's
impact on the interpretation of cathepsin B levels. This analysis underscores the need for careful consideration of
methodological differences when evaluating biomarker data in clinical and research settings.
